customConnectorTests.conclusion: Custom connector calls the North Europe regional proxy. The AWS-facing and ipify-facing calls leave through the North Europe NAT Gateway.
Custom connector calls the West Europe regional proxy. The AWS-facing and ipify-facing calls leave through the West Europe NAT Gateway.

builtInHttpTests.displayName: NAT proof - built-in HTTP direct with response
builtInHttpTests.action: Built-in HTTP GET directly to https://checkip.amazonaws.com/
builtInHttpTests.conclusion: The built-in Power Automate HTTP action works functionally, but direct calls to AWS checkip do not leave through either customer NAT Gateway. This path is not valid for NAT enforcement proof.

builtInHttpTests.displayName: NAT proof - built-in HTTP via regional proxies
builtInHttpTests.action: Built-in HTTP GET to both regional Container Apps proxy /proxy/all endpoints
builtInHttpTests.conclusion: The built-in Power Automate HTTP action can be used safely only when it calls the approved regional proxy endpoints. The customer-controlled proxy then owns the AWS-facing egress through NAT Gateway.

overallConclusion: Power Automate is working end to end when the flow uses the approved regional proxy custom connectors or when the built-in HTTP action calls the approved regional proxy endpoints. Power Automate built-in HTTP direct-to-AWS is functional but bypasses the customer NAT Gateway and must not be treated as an enforced egress path.
